title: King Lear
author: William Shakespeare
content: “As flies to wanton boys, are we to the gods; they kill us for their sport.”
action: Context: The realization of the extreme vulnerability of the individual to forces beyond their influence.

title: The Metamorphosis
author: Franz Kafka
content: “As Gregor Samsa awoke one morning from uneasy dreams he found himself transformed in his bed into a gigantic insect.”
action: Context: The immediate and irreversible shift in an individual's operational utility to the collective.

title: Heart of Darkness
author: Joseph Conrad
action: Context: The final data point encountered when one reaches the absolute boundary of a failed organizational mission.

title: Anna Karenina
author: Leo Tolstoy
content: “Happy families are all alike; every unhappy family is unhappy in its own way.”
action: Context: The observation that success is often a standardized, stable state, while failure is highly unique.

title: Oedipus Rex
author: Sophocles
content: “Call no man happy till he has passed the final boundary of his life and reached the end without a pang of pain.”
action: Context: The acknowledgement that system-wide integrity can only be fully measured at the moment of closure.
